subject: Databases
title: GiST Indexes for Spatial Data
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: GiST Indexes for Spatial Data.

hook.text: Find all restaurants within 5 km. A B-tree can't help — it doesn't understand geographic distance.

buildup.text: GiST (Generalized Search Tree) indexes support geometric and spatial queries. Bounding boxes, ranges, nearest-neighbor.

discovery.text: PostGIS uses GiST indexes to power geographic queries in Postgres. Points, polygons, and distances.

twist.text: GiST also handles range types, IP addresses, and full-text search — an alternative to GIN with different trade-offs.

climax.text: If your data has geometry, proximity, or overlap semantics, GiST is likely the right index.

punchline.text: GiST: the index for spatial and geometric data. B-trees can't do this.

quiz.question: Why can't B-tree indexes handle spatial queries?
options: B-trees only sort on one dimension and can't evaluate geometric relationships like distance
B-trees are too slow for any query
Spatial data cannot be stored in a database
